content: We can reframe a general-purpose interactive agent as a living online meta-learning system where its cumulative, distributed memory consolidation specifically evolves to mitigate 'catastrophic forgetting of behavioral priors'—that is, the loss of well-learned reward-modulated action patterns when the agent adapts to new out-of-distribution environments. This framework treats curiosity-driven exploration (a core capability of most interactive agents) as a natural built-in regularization mechanism that improves memory consolidation for online meta-learning, rather than just a tool for better exploration.
source_topics: Online meta-learning memory consolidation with catastrophic forgetting mitigation
trigger_topic: Online meta-learning memory consolidation with catastrophic forgetting mitigation
